titleStmt.title: The Secretary of State to the Ambassador in Chile ( (Culbertson))

div.head: The Secretary of State to the Ambassador in Chile ( Culbertson )
div.p: 43. Embassy’s 115, September 7, 5 p.m. You should orally inform the Chilean authorities that there is no Federal agency especially charged with responsibility for investigating and studying communist activities in the United States, that this Government regrets that there is not available in the Government service a specialist whose services it could offer, and that it is not in a position to suggest the name of a specialist in private life to undertake the work in question.

As you may know, the Department is interested in ascertaining the full facts with respect to the basis for statements frequently made that alleged communist activities in Latin American countries are directed and controlled from Moscow. You may therefore state to the Chilean authorities that you would be pleased to receive from them for transmission to the Department any information they may be willing to make available to you with respect to the direction or control from beyond the borders of Chile of the activities of persons participating in recent events there and that if the names of the four persons mentioned in your telegram or other persons implicated are communicated to you, you will endeavor to ascertain from your Government what information, if any, it is in a position to supply concerning them.

For your information the Chilean Ambassador in Washington has not yet approached the Department in this matter. Should he do so, the Department will reply as indicated above and furnish him a copy of the report of the “Special Committee to Investigate Communistic Activities in the United States, 71st Congress, 3rd Session” of which the Honorable Hamilton Fish was Chairman.
